recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_high__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'exception_review'. Risk is high and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.80, privilege_level=0.60, internet_exposure=0.90, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it.

strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver kept executable S3.2 profile 's3_bucket_block_public_access_standard' for strategy 's3_bucket_block_public_access_standard'.

dependency_checks.message: Bucket policy is not public and website hosting is disabled; no direct-public-access dependency was detected from runtime probes.

strategies.impact_text: All four bucket-level public access block settings will be enabled for this bucket.
strategies.label: Block public access on bucket

preservation_summary.website_translation_reason: S3 static website hosting is not enabled.

warnings: May break workloads that depend on direct public S3 access. Analyze the affected S3 bucket policy/ACL/public-access-block settings, the bucket KMS key policy/grants (if SSE-KMS), CloudFront OAC/OAI configuration, and any VPC endpoint or cross-account IAM principals that access the bucket. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, dependency validation can be automated.

strategies.label: Migrate to CloudFront + OAC + private S3

preservation_summary.website_translation_reason: S3 static website hosting is not enabled.

warnings: Requires careful validation of bucket policies and KMS permissions. If IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in this account/region, dependency validation can be automated.

dependency_checks.message: Runtime evidence did not confirm active S3 static website hosting for this bucket.

strategies.impact_text: Creates a CloudFront distribution and Route53 alias cutover, removes S3 website hosting, and enables bucket-level public access block settings while serving content from private S3.

fields.description: DNS hostnames that should point to the CloudFront distribution.
Route53 hosted zone ID that owns the website aliases.
ACM certificate ARN for the website aliases. CloudFront certificates must live in us-east-1.
Optional CloudFront default root object override when it should differ from the captured website index document.

strategies.label: Migrate S3 website to CloudFront + private S3

preservation_summary.website_translation_reason: S3 static website hosting is not enabled.

warnings: This strategy changes origin routing and DNS. It is intended only for buckets currently using S3 static website hosting with a simple index/error configuration.
CloudFront alias cutover requires explicit Route53 zone and ACM certificate inputs. Certificates must be compatible with CloudFront.

strategies.decision_rationale: Compatibility resolver defaulted to the compatible profile 's3_keep_public_exception' for strategy 's3_keep_public_exception'.

dependency_checks.message: Public access remains enabled; exception must include business approval and review date.

strategies.impact_text: Bucket-level public access block settings remain unchanged while exception workflow is used.
